metadescription.value: Join Rolex on World Oceans Day as it celebrates those leading by example in efforts to keep our oceans perpetual. Watch the video on rolex.org.

text.value: On World Oceans Day, we are proud to introduce our “Oceans Moment”. Through the company’s development of divers’ watches capable of descending to the deep ocean, we have been at the forefront of marine exploration for more than 70 years, first for the thrill of discovery, and then for the sake of research and protection. Today, the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative lends its support to a wide range of projects in the field of conservation across all of the planet’s landscapes. The “Oceans Moment” is an opportunity to celebrate those leading by example in efforts to understand and protect the Earth’s largest reservoir of life: its seas.

pageCaption.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. Vreni is planning ever-deeper dives to study, understand and preserve this unique biodiversity before it is too late.

metadescription.value: Vreni Häusserman is a German biologist who is exploring the unique marine life of the Patagonian fjords in the south of Chile. These extraordinary and delicate underwater environments are suffering from the effects of intensive fish farming and global warming. pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

pageCaption.value: “Think like an ocean”. Working to protect the iconic Galápagos Islands Marine Reserve, Sylvia Earle and her Mission Blue team led a groundbreaking expedition to assess the health of the ocean after nearly 25 years of protection. What they found could be used as a blueprint of success for the rest of the planet.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 1

metadescription.value: 25 years after the creation of the Galápagos Marine Reserve, an expedition has been launched to collect new data. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue has been helping to track animals in the Galápagos Marine Reserve to analyse their well-being around the archipelago. Through this work, legendary oceanographer Sylvia Earle and a team of scientists are making incredible insights into the connectivity of the Galápagos with the rest of the planet – including discovering DNA sequences that could be completely new to science.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Galápagos Islands Episode 2

metadescription.value: Using eDNA, researchers are aiming to characterize the marine diversity of the Galápagos to protect specific habitats. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative supports those using science to understand the world’s environmental challenges and who are finding ways to restore balance to our ecosystems, particularly the oceans, which are under siege from a host of pressures.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Perpetual Planet - Video

metadescription.value: Through its Perpetual Planet initiative, Rolex supports Mission Blue in protecting marine ecosystems across the world. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Perpetual Planet - Video

pageCaption.value: At 16 years of age, while surfing the crystal waters of his home island in French Polynesia, Titouan Bernicot had an eye-opening experience that led him to dedicate his life to protecting the coral reefs he loves. He founded Coral Gardeners, kick-starting coral restoration on the island alongside his childhood friends. Today these “ocean kids” are inspiring a global community of reef defenders.

metadescription.value: Discover the story of Titouan Bernicot, founder of Coral Gardeners, who has dedicated his life to restoring coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Coral Gardeners: Restoring the reef - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: Cristina Mittermeier is a pioneer of conservation photography. Now, with the support of Rolex’s Perpetual Planet Initiative, she is continuing to take photographs that spark conversations around the ocean and inspire action to protect it.

metadescription.value: A pioneer of conservation photography, Cristina Mittermeier is using her chosen medium to tell the story of the ocean. Watch the video on rolex.org.
